dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8747 IH: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Fine introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To amend the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to include certain curriculum expenses as permissible uses of funds, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026.

section.header: Allowing funds for artificial intelligence curricula

subsection.header: State allowable uses for student instruction
subsection.text: Section 4104(b)(3)(A)(i) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 is amended—

paragraph.text: in subclause (VI) by striking or at the end;
by redesignating subclause (VII) as subclause (VIII); and
by inserting after subclause (VI) the following:

subclause.text: the use of artificial intelligence (as defined in section 5002 of the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 (15 U.S.C. 9401)) in a manner that is safe, effective, and responsible; or

subsection.header: State allowable uses for teachers and school leaders
subsection.text: Section 4104(b)(3)(C) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 is amended—

paragraph.text: in clause (v), by striking and at the end;
in clause (vi) by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

clause.text: providing teachers, paraprofessionals, school librarians and media personnel, specialized instructional support personnel, and administrators with the knowledge and skills to use, and teach the use of, artificial intelligence (as defined in section 5002 of the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 (15 U.S.C. 9401)) safely, effectively, and responsibly, including through professional development.

subsection.header: Local allowable uses for student instruction
subsection.text: Section 4107(a)(3) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (I), by striking or at the end;
by redesignating subparagraph (J) as subparagraph (K); and
by inserting after subparagraph (I) the following:

subparagraph.text: instruction on the use of artificial intelligence (as defined in section 5002 of the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 (15 U.S.C. 9401)) in a manner that is safe, effective, and responsible; or

subsection.header: Local allowable uses for teachers and school leaders
subsection.text: Section 4109(a) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (5), by striking and at the end;
in paragraph (6), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

providing teachers, paraprofessionals, school librarians and media personnel, specialized instructional support personnel, and administrators with the knowledge and skills to use, and teach the use of, artificial intelligence (as defined in section 5002 of the National Artificial Intelligence Initiative Act of 2020 (15 U.S.C. 9401)) safely, effectively, and responsibly, including through professional development.
